summary: Reviewed the multi-frequency recurring charges spec against the provided review pack. The pack contains no changed files or diff output, so the implementation does not satisfy any acceptance criteria yet. The spec is mostly clear on supported frequencies and period labels, but it leaves material edge cases around mid-period creation and frequency changes underspecified.
appears_sound: The spec correctly preserves backwards compatibility by requiring `frequency text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'monthly'` with a constrained value set in `tasks/recurring-charge-frequency-spec.md`.
The spec correctly defines frequency-specific `period_yyyymm`, `period_start`, and `period_end` formats for monthly, quarterly, and annual instances in `tasks/recurring-charge-frequency-spec.md`.
The spec explicitly keeps weekly/custom intervals out of scope, which is consistent with the stated monthly billing cron constraint in `tasks/recurring-charge-frequency-spec.md`.

findings.description: Implementation defect: the review pack contains no changed files, so none of the required database, server action, cron, UI, or type changes have been implemented. This blocks all success criteria for multi-frequency recurring charges.
findings.evidence: The pack states `Changed Files (none detected for this diff range)` and `Diff (main...HEAD) (no diff output)`, while the spec lists five files to modify and twelve acceptance criteria.

findings.description: Implementation defect: there is no migration adding the `frequency` column and CHECK constraint, so quarterly and annual frequencies cannot be persisted safely. This also means existing charges cannot rely on the required default `'monthly'` compatibility behavior.
findings.evidence: The spec requires `supabase/migrations/NEW_add_recurring_frequency.sql`, but the pack shows no changed migration files or SQL diff.

findings.description: Implementation defect: the billing cron changes are absent, so recurring charges would still be generated using the monthly-only period logic described in the problem statement. Quarterly and annual charges therefore cannot be due-gated or assigned correct period labels and date spans.
findings.evidence: The spec requires changes to `src/app/api/cron/oj-projects-billing/route.ts`, including `getRecurringChargePeriod`, but the pack contains no changed cron file.

findings.description: Implementation defect: server actions are not changed to accept, persist, update, or return `frequency`. Even if the database column existed, the UI and API path would be unable to manage it correctly.
findings.evidence: The spec requires changes to `src/app/actions/oj-projects/recurring-charges.ts`, but the pack contains no changed server action file.

findings.description: Implementation defect: the UI form and charge list changes are absent, so users cannot select a frequency or see the saved frequency. This fails the user-facing success criteria even if backend behavior were later added.
findings.evidence: The spec requires changes to `src/app/(authenticated)/oj-projects/clients/page.tsx`, but the pack contains no changed UI file.

findings.description: Unresolved ambiguity: the spec says a quarterly charge created in February first bills for Q1, which implies billing for time before the charge existed. Because pro-rating is out of scope, the intended behavior for mid-period creation needs an explicit business decision.
findings.evidence: The edge case says `If a quarterly charge is created in February, it won't bill until April... it will first appear on the Q1 bill`, while scope says `Pro-rating partial periods` is out of scope.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The product may intentionally bill full calendar periods regardless of creation date, but that policy is not stated clearly enough for implementation or support.
findings.what_would_confirm: Confirm whether non-monthly charges created mid-period should bill the full containing period, wait until the next full period, or use an effective start date.

findings.description: Unresolved ambiguity: changing a charge's frequency mid-period is underspecified because the new period key may not overlap with previously generated monthly keys. The unique constraint prevents exact duplicate period labels but does not prevent double billing the same calendar days under different frequency labels.
findings.evidence: The spec states `If a charge is changed from monthly to quarterly mid-quarter... The unique constraint prevents duplicates`, but the same date range could have monthly labels like `2026-01` and a quarterly label like `2026-Q1`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The business may accept this as manual admin responsibility, but the spec currently implies the database constraint alone solves it.
findings.what_would_confirm: Define whether frequency changes should take effect immediately, from the next unopened period, or require checking overlapping existing instances before generating a new one.

findings.description: Spec defect: invoice period visibility is asserted rather than specified, because the spec says line items use snapshots and that period coverage is visible in invoice notes without requiring a concrete display change. This may leave invoices unclear if the existing invoice rendering does not actually show `period_start` and `period_end`.
findings.evidence: The Invoice Display section says `No changes needed` because the period will be visible via `period_start`/`period_end`, but no changed invoice file or acceptance criterion verifies this rendering.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The existing invoice renderer may already include those fields in notes, but the pack does not include that code.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify the invoice builder renders period start and end for recurring charge instances, or add an explicit acceptance criterion and implementation task.

empty_categories: No security or permission requirement gaps identified within the provided spec beyond the absence of implementation.
No performance-scale concerns found in the spec; frequency filtering is simple monthly cron logic.
No repo-convention conflict found in the stated database default/CHECK constraint approach based on the supplied conventions.
